title: AnomalySignature
description: Anonymous anomaly signature — the ONLY thing the free tier ever transmits. Structurally anonymous: no user, account, path, hostname, or command-line fields exist in this schema, by design. Clients pull the whole known-issues feed and match locally; signatures flow one way. Every transmission is logged byte-for-byte on the client.

platform.description: Sensor platform. Windows is a future sibling sensor speaking this same protocol.

process.description: Identity of the offending process. Abstract by design — pid+starttime is the macOS implementation detail and never leaves the machine.

bundle_id.description: Reverse-DNS bundle identifier when resolvable; null for bare executables.

executable_name.description: Base name only — never a path.

install_source.description: How the binary was installed, derived on-device from its path. The PATH never leaves the machine — only this low-cardinality category, which is safe and enriches fleet analysis (e.g. 'how are people running mysqld').

mode.description: Partner-manifest mode active at observation time (verified sender only), e.g. 'exporting'. Modes raise thresholds; they never exempt.

type.description: Anomaly vocabulary — two valid forms on the wire. (1) Legacy: the original five macOS rule names (sustained_cpu, cputime_ratio, rss_leak, rss_ceiling, novel_process), still emitted by shipped sensors. (2) Universal namespaced form `{dimension}.{pattern}` where dimension is one of cpu|memory|gpu|network|disk|energy|responsiveness — e.g. 'cpu.sustained', 'memory.leak', 'disk.thrash', 'energy.drain'. The namespace is platform-neutral by design: Windows/Linux sensors and new macOS dimensions extend the pattern half without schema changes.

magnitude_curve.description: Downsampled metric curve over the window (percent CPU or MB RSS for the legacy types; the dimension's native unit for namespaced types). Single-point curves are legitimate for ratio/ceiling rules; window rules produce series.

baseline_value.description: Rolling baseline the observation deviated from, same unit as curve.

duration_hours.description: How long the condition has persisted, if pre-existing (cputime_ratio rule).

os_version.description: Marketing version, e.g. '27.0'. Never a build fingerprint.

hardware_class.description: The raw hw.model identifier, lowercased (e.g. 'mac16,5') — never a serial or unique id. The server buckets it to a coarse class with k-anonymity at ingestion; rare models fall back to 'other'.

observed_at.description: Truncated to the hour client-side to resist timing correlation.
